food_subgroup: Chinese rice bowl bundle meal set for two persons, comprising rice, protein (meat or tofu), and vegetable side dishes

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a Chinese rice bowl bundle set for two. FOODON_00002809 (meal food product) is used as the closest parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00FL (Mixed dishes) is the closest parent. No specific code exists for a Chinese rice bowl bundle set for two persons.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code exists for this composite meal set product.

main_ingredients: protein (chicken, pork, or tofu depending on selection)

serving.description: 1 bundle set for 2 persons (approximately 2 rice bowls with protein and vegetable sides)
serving.natural_language_portion: One 2 Pax Bundle Set typically comprises two portions of steamed white rice (approximately 200 g each) with shared protein and vegetable sides, totalling approximately 700 g for two persons.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated from HPB Singapore Foodo composite: Steamed White Rice, Braised Chicken, Stir-fried Vegetables (Chinese meal set analogue)
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board (HPB) Singapore — Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single HPB Foodo entry exists for a 2 Pax Bundle Set. Values are estimated by compositing HPB Foodo entries for steamed white rice (per 100 g), braised chicken (per 100 g), and stir-fried leafy vegetables (per 100 g) in proportions representative of a Chinese rice bowl set meal: approximately 57% steamed rice, 29% protein (braised chicken), and 14% vegetables. All component values are drawn from HPB Foodo approved entries. Per-100 g values represent the blended composite of the full 700 g set divided by 7.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of white rice and mixed Chinese meal analogues — University of Sydney GI Database; Steamed white rice GI reference (Atkinson et al., 2008)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a 2 Pax Bundle Set. GI of 68 is estimated using the University of Sydney GI Database reference for steamed white rice (GI ~72) as the dominant carbohydrate source, moderated downward to 68 to account for the protein and fat content of the braised chicken and vegetable components, which slow gastric emptying and reduce the overall glycemic response of the mixed meal. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (68 × 150.5) / 100 ≈ 102, where carbohydrate per 700 g serving = 21.5 × 7 = 150.5 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content approximately 310 mg per 100 g; full set meal delivers approximately 2170 mg sodium for two persons (approximately 1085 mg per person), approaching the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000 mg
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should request reduced-sodium sauces and limit soy-based condiments when consuming this set meal, as the braising sauces and seasoning contribute substantially to the sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load per serving (approximately 102 for the full set; approximately 51 per person) driven by the large steamed white rice component with GI of approximately 68
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consider substituting steamed white rice with brown rice or reducing the rice portion, and pairing with additional non-starchy vegetables to lower the overall glycemic load of the meal.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat content (approximately 1.1 g per 100 g) and elevated sodium from braising sauces; cholesterol approximately 38 mg per 100 g from animal protein component
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should choose lean protein options (tofu or steamed chicken without skin) and request reduced-oil preparation to lower saturated fat and sodium intake from this set meal.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The 2 Pax Bundle Set is a Chinese-cuisine meal set designed for two persons, served at First Bowl Rice Kitchen in Singapore. It typically comprises two portions of steamed white rice accompanied by a shared protein dish (such as braised chicken, pork, or tofu) and one or more vegetable side dishes (such as stir-fried leafy greens or seasonal vegetables), all prepared in a Chinese home-style or zi char cooking tradition. The meal is assembled in a bento or rice bowl format and is representative of everyday Singaporean Chinese comfort food. Nutritionally, the set is a balanced mixed meal providing moderate energy (approximately 148 kcal per 100 g), meaningful protein from the meat or tofu component, and carbohydrates predominantly from steamed white rice. The sodium content is moderately elevated due to soy-based braising sauces and seasoning. The glycemic index is estimated at 68 (medium), reflecting the dominant white rice component moderated by the protein and fat from the accompanying dishes. Clinically, the meal is of moderate concern for individuals managing hypertension (due to sodium), Type 2 Diabetes (due to glycemic load from white rice), and cardiovascular disease (due to saturated fat and sodium). The set is dairy-free and suitable for those avoiding dairy, but contains soy and possible gluten from soy sauce. It is a practical, filling, and culturally familiar meal option for Singaporean diners seeking a complete Chinese rice bowl experience for two.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for a 2 Pax Bundle Set as a named dish. Nutrient values are estimated by compositing HPB Foodo entries for steamed white rice, braised chicken, and stir-fried vegetables in representative proportions. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database using steamed white rice as the primary glycemic driver, moderated for the mixed-meal effect. Confidence is medium because the exact composition of the bundle set varies by restaurant selection and is not standardised.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo component entries used for this composite estimate. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo component entries used for this composite estimate. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo component entries used for this composite estimate. Trans fat (trans_fat_g) is 0.0 as Chinese home-style cooking does not use partially hydrogenated oils; confirmed negligible per HPB Foodo component data. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and all other reported micronutrients are estimated from HPB Foodo component proportions. The 2 Pax Bundle Set is a restaurant-specific composite product from First Bowl Rice Kitchen (Funan, Singapore); exact composition may vary by order. Glycemic load per serving is calculated for the full 700 g set (two persons); per-person glycemic load is approximately 51. The halal status is not confirmed for this restaurant; consumers requiring halal certification should verify directly with the restaurant.
